This experiment has implications for how developers evaluate and choose models for their applications.

articles.title: RFC 10008: The new HTTP Query Method
articles.summary: The HTTP QUERY method allows a client to request that the server process the enclosed content in a safe and idempotent manner, similar to POST requests. This is achieved by including an Accept-Query header field with the query string, which specifies the processing target for the enclosed content. The method also includes several response fields, such as Content-Location Response Field and Location Response Field, that provide information about the result of the processing.

articles.title: Color picking Oklch for mortals
articles.summary: Oklch is a tool that allows users to pick colors with perceptual accuracy, meaning it takes into account how colors appear to humans in different lighting conditions. This means that setting the Lightness to 60% in Oklch will result in colors that are perceived as 60% bright by humans, regardless of the color chosen. The perception aspect of picking colors is key, and this is limited by our ability to see colors in dark situations or very bright situations. Chroma, which refers to the amount of color left after removing grayscale, can vary depending on the monitor and lighting conditions.

articles.title: Can gzip be a language model?
articles.summary: A compressor such as gzip can be used to generate language models by predicting the probability of each word in a given text based on its context. This is achieved through the compression-prediction equivalence, where every prediction model is inherently a compressor and all compression algorithms are prediction models. The algorithm uses a sliding window of 32 KiB to find matches against the recent text in the window, encoding continuation that echoes something already in the window as a cheap back-reference instead of literal bytes. This allows for the generation of coherent text with high probability, making it possible to prime a model and generate language models such as tiny Shakespeare.
